Technology.thesis: The AI capex cycle continues to be the dominant driver, fueling demand across semiconductors, hardware, and software. Hyperscaler investments and new AI applications are creating a strong tailwind, though some software names face valuation scrutiny.
watchFor: Mag 7 hyperscaler capex guide DOWN at next earnings — would crack the AI demand thesis
Core CPI print > 0.4% MoM for two consecutive months — forces hawkish Fed repricing, hurting growth multiples
China export controls expand to advanced AI chips or equipment — would disrupt supply chain

Consumer Cyclical.thesis: Retail is mixed with discount stores showing strength, while specialty retail faces headwinds. Auto manufacturers are navigating mixed signals, and residential construction shows opportunity despite broader economic concerns.
watchFor: Consumer confidence index drops below 90 for two consecutive months
Interest rates rise unexpectedly, impacting housing and auto loans
Unemployment rate rises above 4.5% — signals broader consumer weakness

Communication Services.thesis: Internet content and entertainment companies are navigating AI integration and content monetization strategies, leading to varied sentiment. Social media platforms are seeing new app launches and strong earnings.
watchFor: Major tech company reports significant slowdown in advertising revenue
Increased regulatory pressure on social media content
New streaming service subscriber growth stalls across the industry

Industrials.thesis: Aerospace & Defense is seeing strong backlogs, while Integrated Freight & Logistics benefits from strategic spin-offs. Farm & Heavy Construction Machinery faces headwinds from agricultural weakness.
watchFor: Global geopolitical tensions ease significantly — reduces defense spending
Industrial production index declines for two consecutive months
Fuel prices spike, impacting logistics and heavy machinery operating costs

Healthcare.thesis: The GLP-1 obesity drug wave continues to drive significant upside for leaders, while healthcare plans are seeing positive analyst sentiment. Medical devices face a demand slowdown.
watchFor: New GLP-1 drug entrants with superior efficacy/safety profiles
Increased regulatory scrutiny on drug pricing
Healthcare policy changes negatively impact insurance providers

Financials.thesis: Banks are showing mixed signals, with some anticipating strong trading revenue while others see stock drops. Credit services are bullish, and asset management firms are attracting positive analyst attention.
watchFor: Federal Reserve signals unexpected rate cut/hike — impacts bank net interest margins
Significant increase in corporate defaults or consumer delinquencies
New financial regulations negatively impact profitability

Consumer Defensive.thesis: Consumer defensive stocks are showing positive momentum, with Estee Lauder seeing strong gains and positive analyst sentiment.
watchFor: Significant downturn in consumer discretionary spending
Inflationary pressures erode consumer purchasing power
EL reports weaker-than-expected sales growth

Utilities.thesis: Independent power producers are showing strong performance with earnings beats and analyst upgrades, indicating a positive outlook for the sector.
watchFor: Unexpected rise in natural gas prices impacts power generation costs
Regulatory changes negatively affect independent power producers
VST reports weaker-than-expected earnings

Basic Materials.thesis: The industrial metals and mining sector is facing mixed signals, with geopolitical concerns and insider sales impacting some companies despite strategic partnerships.
watchFor: Global industrial demand significantly declines
New supply sources disrupt commodity markets
Geopolitical tensions ease, reducing demand for strategic materials

Precious Metals.thesis: Precious metals are facing headwinds as outflows from gold and bitcoin ETFs suggest investors are moving away from the 'debasement trade,' despite some analysts projecting higher gold prices.
watchFor: US Dollar Index (DXY) weakens significantly
Geopolitical tensions escalate, increasing safe-haven demand
Unexpectedly high inflation prints (e.g., Core CPI > 0.4% MoM) for several months
